pipeline_name: Ethereum Block Height vs. Elapsed Time Analysis
research_question: What is the empirical relationship between Ethereum block height and wall-clock elapsed time on Ethereum mainnet since the merge?

analysis_period.rationale: Post-Merge (The Merge occurred on block 15537393, 2022-09-15 13:42:42 UTC). Before this, Ethereum used Proof-of-Work with variable block times.

primary.rationale: Allium provides decoded, cross-chain data with SQL interface. Ethereum mainnet blocks are fully indexed post-merge with high uptime.
primary.rate_limits: Plan-dependent; recommend caching daily aggregates to minimize API calls
primary.cost: Enterprise pricing; free tier may require tiered approach (sample first)

secondary.rationale: Excellent for Ethereum data; slightly more latency than Allium but comparable quality. Public SQL editor for exploration.

tertiary.rationale: Block-level data available via ethBlockNumber and eth_getBlockByNumber. Limited for large-scale extraction due to rate limits and no direct SQL.
tertiary.use_case: Validation of pipeline output; not suitable for primary extraction

archive_node.source_name: Full archive node (via Infura, Alchemy, or self-hosted)
archive_node.rationale: Direct access to immutable block data; lower latency but requires operational overhead. Useful for real-time monitoring or validation.
archive_node.trade_off: High infrastructure cost vs. no API rate limits

fields.description: Block height on Ethereum mainnet. Deterministic, immutable identifier.
Unix timestamp (seconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC) of the block. Set by the proposer; subject to validator hardware clock.
fields.notes: Post-Merge blocks have ~12-second average spacing but are not perfectly regular. Timestamps are monotonically increasing per Ethereum protocol rules.

fields.description: Unix timestamp in seconds (alternative representation for clarity).
ISO 8601 human-readable datetime (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS UTC). Derived from block_timestamp.
Seconds elapsed since Ethereum genesis block (block 0, timestamp 1438269973, 2015-07-30 03:26:13 UTC). Derived field: block_timestamp - 1438269973.
Days elapsed since The Merge (block 15537393). Derived: (block_timestamp - merge_timestamp) / 86400.
Block count since genesis (block 0). Derived: block_number.
fields.notes: Equivalent to block_number for post-genesis blocks. Useful for calculating average block time.

fields.description: Block count since The Merge. Derived: block_number - 15537393.
Seconds between this block and the previous block. Derived: block_timestamp - LAG(block_timestamp) OVER (ORDER BY block_number).
fields.notes: Null for block 0 and block 15537394 (first block in sample). Post-Merge Ethereum targets 12 seconds; actual values range 0–20+ due to validator randomness and network conditions.

fields.description: Total gas consumed by all transactions in the block.
fields.use_case: Control variable for block complexity; heavier blocks may see minor timestamp adjustments

fields.description: Maximum gas allowed in the block. Adjusts dynamically based on network congestion.
Number of transactions included in the block.
fields.use_case: Descriptor of block activity; may correlate with minor timestamp drift

fields.description: Address of the block proposer (validator). Anonymized address.
fields.notes: Post-Merge, this field represents the block proposer; not relevant for consensus but useful for validator clustering analysis.

fields.description: Base fee in wei per unit gas (EIP-1559). Burned by the protocol.
Proof-of-Work difficulty. Post-Merge (block 15537394+), this field is set to 0 (PoS consensus).
fields.notes: Not useful for post-Merge analysis; retained for historical completeness

fields.description: Keccak-256 hash of the parent block header. Verifies chain continuity.
fields.use_case: Validation: confirm each block's parent is the prior block

fields.description: Keccak-256 hash of the block header. Immutable identifier.
Metadata: when this record was extracted from the data source. Useful for version control and replication audits.

block_completeness.description: Verify no blocks are skipped. For the period [start_block, end_block], expected_row_count = (end_block - start_block + 1).
block_completeness.sql_template: SELECT COUNT(*) as actual_count, (MAX(block_number) - MIN(block_number) + 1) as expected_count FROM ethereum_block_data WHERE block_number >= 15537394;
block_completeness.failure_action: Alert: Missing block(s). Investigate data source outage or API lag.

timestamp_monotonicity.description: block_timestamp must increase with block_number. No duplicate or out-of-order timestamps.
timestamp_monotonicity.sql_template: SELECT COUNT(*) as violations FROM ethereum_block_data WHERE LAG(block_timestamp) OVER (ORDER BY block_number) >= block_timestamp;
timestamp_monotonicity.failure_action: Alert: Protocol violation or data corruption. Investigate data source.
timestamp_monotonicity.rationale: Ethereum protocol enforces monotonic timestamps (EIP-1559). Violations indicate data integrity issues.

timestamp_realism.description: Timestamps should fall within [genesis_time, current_time] and not deviate by >1 hour from expected drift.
timestamp_realism.sql_template: SELECT block_number, block_timestamp, ABS(block_timestamp - (1438269973 + (block_number * 12))) as drift_seconds FROM ethereum_block_data WHERE ABS(block_timestamp - (1438269973 + (block_number * 12))) > 3600;
timestamp_realism.failure_action: Alert: Large timestamp anomalies. Possible data corruption or validator attack (timestomping).
timestamp_realism.rationale: Post-Merge target block time is 12 seconds. Drift >1 hour is unrealistic.

block_time_distribution.check_name: Inter-block times are within expected range
block_time_distribution.description: Calculate time_since_previous_block_seconds. Post-Merge mean ~12s, min 0s (uncle blocks), max ~20–30s (validator randomness).
block_time_distribution.sql_template: SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY time_since_previous_block_seconds) as median_time, PERCENTILE_CONT(0.99) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY time_since_previous_block_seconds) as p99_time FROM ethereum_block_data WHERE block_number > 15537394;

expected_values.max_observed: 60+ seconds (rare, due to validator queue dynamics)

block_time_distribution.failure_action: Alert if p99 > 60s consistently. Investigate validator participation or data source lag.

derived_field_consistency.description: Verify elapsed_seconds_from_genesis and elapsed_days_from_merge match expectations.
derived_field_consistency.sql_template: SELECT COUNT(*) as consistency_failures FROM ethereum_block_data WHERE ABS((block_timestamp - 1438269973) - elapsed_seconds_from_genesis) > 0 OR ABS(((block_timestamp - 1663340827) / 86400) - elapsed_days_from_merge) > 0.0001;
derived_field_consistency.failure_action: Alert: Calculation errors in ETL pipeline. Review derivation logic.

null_value_analysis.description: Check for nulls in non-nullable fields and unexpected nulls in optional fields.
null_value_analysis.sql_template: SELECT COUNT(*) as null_block_numbers FROM ethereum_block_data WHERE block_number IS NULL; SELECT COUNT(*) as null_timestamps FROM ethereum_block_data WHERE block_timestamp IS NULL;
null_value_analysis.failure_action: Alert: Null values in mandatory fields. Data source outage or schema mismatch.

duplicate_detection.description: Verify block_number is unique. Each block appears exactly once.
duplicate_detection.sql_template: SELECT block_number, COUNT(*) as cnt FROM ethereum_block_data GROUP BY block_number HAVING cnt > 1;
duplicate_detection.failure_action: Alert: Duplicate rows. Rerun extraction with deduplication.

schema_validation.check_name: Data types and ranges are correct
schema_validation.description: Confirm block_number is non-negative integer, block_timestamp is in valid Unix range.
schema_validation.sql_template: SELECT COUNT(*) as invalid_blocks FROM ethereum_block_data WHERE block_number < 0 OR block_timestamp < 1438269973 OR block_timestamp > UNIX_TIMESTAMP();
schema_validation.failure_action: Alert: Invalid data types or out-of-range values. Data corruption or parsing error.

lag_detection.description: Confirm latest block is within 100 blocks of current network tip. Current tip can be fetched via eth_blockNumber RPC call.
lag_detection.sql_template: SELECT MAX(block_number) as latest_extracted_block FROM ethereum_block_data; COMPARE TO eth_blockNumber RPC endpoint.
lag_detection.failure_action: Alert: Data is stale. Data source may be behind network tip.

observation_period.rationale: Post-Merge period captures PoS consensus behavior. 2+ years provides sufficient temporal variation for time-series analysis and seasonal patterns.

block_range.rationale: Block 15537394 is the first PoS block (post-Merge). Block 19124999 estimated as of 2024-12-31 (rough estimate: ~100 blocks/sec * 86400 sec/day * 843 days ≈ 7.3M blocks; actual ~8.4M blocks in period)
block_range.calculation: Ethereum targets 1 block per 12 seconds = 7,200 blocks per day. Over 843 days: 843 * 7200 = 6,069,600 blocks (lower bound; actual is ~7.3–8.4M due to slot inclusions and network variations)

temporal_resolution.rationale: Block-level granularity enables precise time-series analysis, detection of network events (congestion, reorganizations), and inter-block time distribution analysis.
temporal_resolution.alternative_aggregations: Daily/weekly available on demand for lighter-weight analysis

expected_characteristics.timestamps_per_day: 86,400 seconds; thus ~12 blocks per unique second on average
expected_characteristics.implied_block_time_mean: 4367520 seconds / 6069600 blocks ≈ 0.72 seconds per block (over the full period, accounting for network variations)

extraction_strategy.approach: Incremental extract with full daily snapshot validation

phase_1_feasibility.description: Extract first 1,000 blocks post-Merge to validate schema and data quality.
phase_1_feasibility.sql: SELECT * FROM ethereum.core.blocks WHERE block_number BETWEEN 15537394 AND 15538393 ORDER BY block_number;
phase_1_feasibility.purpose: Schema validation, timestamp verification, confirm data source accessibility

phase_2_sample.description: Extract representative sample: one week of data (7,200 blocks).
phase_2_sample.sql: SELECT * FROM ethereum.core.blocks WHERE block_timestamp BETWEEN 1663340827 AND 1663946027 ORDER BY block_number;
phase_2_sample.purpose: Estimate extraction rate, validate data quality pipeline, estimate final storage size

phase_3_full_extract.description: Full extraction for analysis period (2022-09-15 to 2024-12-31).
phase_3_full_extract.sql: SELECT block_number, block_timestamp, gas_used, gas_limit, transaction_count, base_fee_per_gas, parent_hash, block_hash, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP as extraction_timestamp FROM ethereum.core.blocks WHERE block_timestamp BETWEEN 1663340827 AND 1735689600 ORDER BY block_number;
phase_3_full_extract.batch_processing: Recommend batching by month (604,800 seconds ≈ 7 days * 86,400) to manage memory and API quotas
phase_3_full_extract.expected_runtime: ~5–15 minutes (depending on API plan and concurrency)
phase_3_full_extract.parallelization: Safe to parallelize monthly batches; use unique output files per month, then UNION ALL

steps.sql: time_since_previous_block_seconds = block_timestamp - LAG(block_timestamp) OVER (ORDER BY block_number)

output_format.partitioning: By month (block_timestamp YEAR-MONTH) for efficient querying
output_format.compression: Snappy or gzip (Parquet native or CSV)

data_governance.data_availability: Public. Ethereum block data is fully transparent and immutable.
data_governance.licensing: Public domain (blockchain data is not copyrightable).
data_governance.retention_policy: Indefinite. Once extracted, data should be retained for reproducibility.
data_governance.version_control: Include extraction_timestamp and data_source version in metadata.
data_governance.reproducibility: All query SQL and extraction parameters documented for audit trail.

key_variables_for_regression.interpretation: Wall-clock elapsed time; measures systematic relationship
Residual inter-block time; useful for examining deviations from model
Block complexity; may correlate with minor timestamp drift

regression_model_sketch.rationale: Block height and wall-clock time should be nearly perfectly collinear (R² ≈ 0.9999). β₁ estimates the average seconds per block over the full period.
regression_model_sketch.identification: No endogeneity (block_number is deterministic, not subject to simultaneity bias)

robustness_checks.rationale: Timestamp drift may vary with gas_used or transaction_count
Distribution of inter-block times may be non-normal; quantiles capture tail behavior
Detect if any single month drives result; identify structural breaks
Account for serial correlation in inter-block times
